insight: Iran's performance in the 2026 World Cup has shown a notable trend when it comes to hydration breaks, with the team gaining momentum in both of their breaks so far, resulting in a mean increase of 0.200 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have been beneficial for Iran, allowing them to regroup and come back stronger. Notably, in the instances where Iran was not pressing before the break, they were able to maintain this positive momentum, with an average increase of 0.200 points per minute, indicating that the team is able to capitalize on the brief pause in play to refocus and push forward. This ability to gain momentum after hydration breaks could be a key factor in Iran's success in the tournament, as it allows them to stay competitive and potentially turn the tide of a match in their favor.
